description_text: are you someone who enjoys solving problems and making things run smoothly behind the scenes? we’re looking for a  technical analyst  to join our team and support a dynamic client in the vacation rental industry. in this role, you will help investigate technical and data-related issues, support users, and ensure pricing accuracy and synchronization across connected platforms. the responsibilities below outline the core focus areas of this role. we offer a  hybrid working arrangement , combining remote flexibility with in-office collaboration to help you stay productive while staying connected with the team. what you'll be doing handling technical issues (reactive) investigate technical and data-related issues reported by users or internal teams. you will analyze problems, replicate scenarios, identify root causes, and propose clear solutions or requirements to support implementation by the development or related teams. ensuring pricing accuracy & system alignment work closely with data and integration processes to ensure pricing, fees, taxes, and related information remain accurate and consistently synchronized across connected systems and partners. you will help investigate discrepancies, validate data flows, and support corrective actions when needed. proactive monitoring & improvement regularly review system behavior and data patterns to identify potential inconsistencies or areas for improvement. you will highlight findings and suggest preventive steps to improve reliability and data quality. documentation document your findings, processes, and resolutions clearly to support knowledge sharing and reduce recurring issues. team collaboration collaborate with cross-functional teams to validate solutions, clarify requirements, and resolve issues in a structured and timely manner. what you bring -  a curious and analytical mind -  you enjoy understanding how systems work and figuring out why something behaves a certain way. -  clear communication (english & bahasa indonesia) -  you can explain technical topics in a clear and structured way, both verbally and in writing. -  technical skills -  experience with sql, apis, and google workspace is required. familiarity with python, php, or aws is a plus. -  team-oriented attitude -  you are comfortable working with others and sharing information openly. -  organized working style -  you keep track of your work and maintain clear documentation. your background 1. a degree in computer science, information technology, engineering, or a related technical field. 2. at least 1 year of experience in tech support, software development, qa/testing, saas, or a similar environment. 3. a willingness to learn, take initiative, and grow in a structured technical role.

description_text: key responsibilities: - design , build and manage data warehouse / data lake. - design, build, and maintain data pipelines from multiple companies/business units into a centralized data warehouse. - develop and manage etl/elt processes to ensure data is accurate, clean, and available for reporting. - build and maintain data warehouse, data marts, and structured datasets for business intelligence needs. - integrate data from various sources such as accounting systems, internal systems, databases, apis, excel, and google sheets. - perform data cleaning, transformation, validation, and standardization across business units. - monitor pipeline performance, data freshness, data quality, and system reliability. - collaborate with bi engineer and business stakeholders to prepare data for dashboards and management reports. - implement data security, access control, backup, and documentation for data architecture and pipelines. - troubleshoot data issues and ensure consistency between source systems and the data warehouse. qualifications: - bachelor’s degree (s1) in computer science, information systems, data engineering, statistics, mathematics, engineering, or related fields. - minimum 2 years of experience as data engineer, etl developer, database engineer, or similar role. - strong proficiency in sql and relational databases such as postgresql, mysql, sql server, bigquery, snowflake, or redshift. - experience in etl/elt processes, data warehouse design, and data modeling. - proficiency in python for data processing and automation. - experience with api integration, data ingestion, and workflow orchestration tools such as airflow, prefect, dagster, or similar. - familiarity with cloud platforms. - strong understanding of data quality, data governance, and database performance optimization. - strong analytical thinking, problem-solving skills, and attention to detail. - able to work with cross-functional teams and understand business data needs.

description_text: responsibilities: 1. assessment & strategy • discovery: conduct deep-dive discovery sessions to map application dependencies, database schemas, and networking requirements using tools like aws application discovery service. • migration planning: determine the optimal migration strategy (the 6 rs: rehost, replatform, refactor, retire, retain, or repurchase) for each workload. • tco analysis: provide technical input for total cost of ownership (tco) reports to justify migration paths. 2. execution & execution • migration tooling: lead the hands-on execution of migrations using aws application migration service (mgn), aws database migration service (dms), and aws datasync. • modernization: refactor monolithic applications into containerized microservices using amazon eks or ecs. • infrastructure as code (iac): automate the provisioning of destination environments using terraform, aws cdk, or cloudformation. 3. networking & security • connectivity: establish secure migration tunnels via aws client vpn, site-tosite vpn, or direct connect. • identity migration: migrate and integrate identity providers (ldap/active directory) with aws iam and iam identity center. • compliance: ensure all migrated workloads meet industry standards (hipaa, pci-dss, soc2) within the aws shared responsibility model. 4. validation & cutover • testing: perform uat (user acceptance testing) and performance benchmarking to ensure the application performs better in aws than its source environment. • cutover management: execute final data syncs and dns switchovers (using route 53) with minimal downtime. • post-migration support: provide "hypercare" support during the first 30 days post-migration to tune performance

description_text:
at vida we’re building the future of digital identity. as a data engineer you’ll work across the stack—from platform and infrastructure to data pipelines and end-user tooling. you’ll help us modernize our batch etls and lead our push into streaming and real-time fraud detection. we run on aws and make extensive use of scala spark, dbt, and terraform. responsibilities build and expose the data lake/lakehouse so teams can understand business and product performance and make better decisions. 
 
 design and evolve a data platform that enables scientists and analysts to query, model, and ship features and product metrics. 
 develop scalable and reliable batch and streaming pipelines that ingest from varied sources and land trusted datasets. 
 maintain and fix existing etls, improving reliability, observability, and cost while planning safe backfills and replays. 
 model curated layers and maintain transformation logic (e.g., dbt), including testing, documentation, and data quality. 
 build with infrastructure as code (e.g., terraform) and contribute to ci/cd and deployment practices. 
 partner with engineering, and data science to deliver low-latency signals and features for real-time decisioning. 
 contribute to internal tools, reusable components, and simple dashboards that unblock self-service. 
 requirements 
 solid engineering foundation with 5+ years of relevant experience in data engineering and/or distributed systems. 
 hands-on work building data pipelines (batch and/or streaming) using at least one distributed framework (e.g., spark, kafka, flink). 
 comfortable with sql and a transformation framework (e.g., dbt or similar). 
 familiarity with aws services for data (any mix of s3, emr/glue, kinesis/msk, lambda, redshift/athena) or equivalent cloud experience. 
 exposure to workflow orchestration (e.g., airflow). 
 experience using terraform (or similar iac) to manage data/platform resources. 
 ownership mindset, focus on reliability, and enjoyment of collaborating in a fast-moving environment. 
 
 what are we trying to solve? billions of people still cannot securely prove their identity. without it, they’re shut out of education, healthcare, finance, and civic participation. we believe this must change—and that digital identity should be trustworthy, private, and accessible to everyone. 
 at vida, we’re creating a frictionless digital identity system that meets the needs of our time and works anywhere, for everyone. 
 why are we solving this problem? global initiatives like the un and world bank’s id4d aim to provide legal identity for all by 2030. we expect digital identity to become a basic right, and we want vida to help lead that future. 
 who are we? we’re a mission-driven, pragmatic team motivated by real-world impact—from preventing fraud and corruption to unlocking fair access to essential services. if that resonates with you, we’d love to talk.
